methodology_sections: 1.1 Asset Inventory - Financial assets, state assets, privilege assets, reputation assets
1.3 System Architecture Mapping - Contract hierarchies, dependencies, upgrade patterns, access control
2.1 Entry Point Enumeration - Public/external functions, callbacks, parameter analysis

financial_assets: ERC-20 userToken balances held by FeeAMM per pool (pools[poolId].reserveUserToken; actual token balances in contract)
ERC-20 validatorToken balances held by FeeAMM per pool (pools[poolId].reserveValidatorToken; actual token balances in contract)
LP position value represented by liquidityBalances[poolId][provider] and totalSupply[poolId] (redeemable pro-rata claim on both reserves)
Swap fee spreads embedded in constants M (0.3% fee) and N (0.15% fee), accruing implicitly to LPs via reserve changes
state_assets: pools[poolId]: Pool{reserveUserToken,reserveValidatorToken} accounting state per (userToken, validatorToken) pair
totalSupply[poolId]: total LP shares outstanding per poolId
liquidityBalances[poolId][account]: LP share balances per provider per poolId
Pool identifier derivation: getPoolId(userToken,validatorToken)=keccak256(abi.encode(userToken,validatorToken)) (no canonical ordering enforced)
privilege_assets: None (no owner/admin roles, no pausing, no upgrades, no privileged fee recipient)
reputation_assets: LP share supply and balances as representation of liquidity provider stake and implied governance/reputation (off-chain/accounting trust in reserves vs actual balances)
Validator operational trust: correct execution of swaps impacts protocol credibility (no on-chain reputation primitive)

system_architecture.inheritance_hierarchy: FeeAMM is a standalone contract with no inheritance. Imports IERC20 interface from OpenZeppelin but does not inherit any OpenZeppelin base modules (Ownable/Pausable/ReentrancyGuard/etc.).
external_dependencies: @openzeppelin/contracts/token/ERC20/IERC20.sol (interface only; relies on token implementations for transfer/transferFrom behavior)
system_architecture.upgrade_patterns: None. No proxy/diamond/UUPS patterns present; no initializer; immutable logic once deployed.
system_architecture.access_control_structure: No access control. All key state-mutating functions (executeFeeSwap, rebalanceSwap, mint, burn) are permissionless. Authorization is only via ERC-20 allowance checks enforced by external token contracts during transferFrom.

upgradeability_surface.notes: Single standalone contract; no proxy/diamond; upgradeability not present.
